issue.title: Use generated request adapter for WP_Http::request HEAD redirection default

artifacts.role: WPHX-312.83 runner installing compiler-emitted class-wp-http.php request shell
typed public shell declaration with request, processHeaders, normalize_cookies, and block_request adapters
WordPress-profile adapter IR for request HEAD redirection and normalize_cookies support
HEAD redirection candidate manifest with generated-shell and IR feature evidence
ownership manifest marking the boundary as compiler_emitted_original_path_shell
behavior receipt for the HEAD redirection default candidate

claims: WPHX-312.83 now consumes the compiler-emitted original-path WP_Http::request shell instead of a copied/runner-patched class-wp-http.php body.
The HEAD redirection default helper remains the bounded Haxe-owned branch claim, with helper modules compiled for the generated shell's runtime calls.
The shared generated request shell now also emits processHeaders and normalize_cookies adapters needed by the exercised request paths.
Both deterministic HEAD redirection oracle/candidate probes remain behavior-identical.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership.
This does not claim cookie normalization internals beyond the generated public adapter shape exercised by this candidate.
This does not claim live network behavior, redirect following, upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity, or installed distribution behavior.
This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
